title: Cnemidaria petiolata
intro: Cnemidaria petiolata, also known as the Ceylon fern or Ceylon brake, is a species of fern in the family Cnemidaria, native to Sri Lanka and parts of India. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cnemidaria petiolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cnemidaria petiolata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or processed into a powder or capsule for use in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cnemidaria petiolata is a species of fern in the family Cnemidaria, but more broadly within the larger group of plants known as the Lycopodiophyta, and then further classified within the class Lycopodiopsida and division Lycopodiophyta. The plant falls under the phylum Lycopodiophyta, a group of vascular plants that also includes club mosses and quillworts. Its classification is also associated with the superdivision Streptophyta, which is a group of plants with vascular tissue. The plant's classification can be broken down further into the kingdom Plantae. Overall, the classification of Cnemidaria petiolata is a key aspect of understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
